description: Code-site coverage map for issue #236 Check C (scripts/check-spec-drift.sh). THREE sections back the four C.3/C.4/C.5 sub-checks plus the P1.1 variable-write guard. (1) `code_sites` pins every code-bearing transition in transitions.json to a grep-stable anchor (C.3): an orphaned key (deleted/renamed transition row) or a stale anchor fails CI. (2) `sites` is the per-PHYSICAL-write-site manifest (C.4 + C.5): one entry per literal label-write site, each with its `file`, `movement` (sorted-removes|sorted-adds), and a grep-UNIQUE `anchor` literal that sits within +-8 lines of that write. C.4 reconciles the count of discovered sites per (file, movement) against this manifest; C.5 verifies each manifest anchor is unique in its file AND a write of its movement is adjacent to it — so RELOCATING a write within the same file (same movement, count unchanged) moves it away from its unique anchor and fails CI. (3) `variable_write_allowlist` lists the only two code sites where a variable-valued `--add/--remove-label "$var"` is legitimate (the label_swap helper definition + the hygiene strip loop over a hard-coded label list); ANY other variable-valued write in the four pipeline files is a hard FAIL (P1.1), not a NOTE — a variable write can introduce an undeclared label invisibly. Keyed on grep-stable literals, NEVER line numbers. Maintained BY HAND alongside transitions.json + the code (same PR). The companion unit test is tests/unit/test-spec-drift.sh (TC-SPEC-GATE-037..039 C.3, 042..044 C.4, 045..047 C.5/P1.1).

dispatch-5b-dead-pr-nonewcommits.anchor: no new commits since last review

dispatch-5b-dead-no-pr.anchor: Task appears to have crashed (no PR found)

dispatch-pending-dev-crashed-session-ret.anchor: reviewed FAILED (${cause}) with no live wrapper

dev-trap-success-pr.anchor: PR found: move to pending-review for the review agent

review-no-pr.anchor: a failed label transition is diagnosable

review-bot-missing-fail.anchor: reviewDecision is CHANGES_REQUESTED until the bot review is present

review-ci-rollup-failed.anchor: failed CI check — dev must fix and push

review-token-budget-projection-unavailab.anchor: Token-budget issue projection unavailable — re-queuing for re-review

dispatch-review-token-budget-retry.anchor: Accounting-start refusal and unavailable-hold paths deliberately leave

variable_write_allowlist._comment: P1.1 — the code sites in the five pipeline files where a variable-valued --add/--remove-label "$var" is legitimate (currently NONE — see the #331 note below). Each entry's `anchor` must grep in `file` and is the function whose body holds the dynamic write. Every OTHER variable-valued label write is a hard FAIL (a variable write can introduce an undeclared label the literal-site checks never see). NOTE (#283): label_swap() NO LONGER holds a variable label write — its body now delegates to itp_transition_state (the [INV-87] ITP transition verb), whose byte-identical `gh issue edit … --add-label "$add"` leaf lives in providers/itp-github.sh, which is OUTSIDE the five scanned PIPELINE_FILES. So label_swap() is no longer an allowlist site here; its $remove/$add still come from the LITERAL label_swap callers that C.1–C.5 validate. NOTE (#331, [INV-97]): hygiene_strip_residual_labels() ALSO no longer holds a variable `--remove-label "$t"` loop — it now builds a CSV from its hard-coded transitional-label set (`$remove_csv`) and delegates to `itp_transition_state "$issue_num" "$remove_csv" ""`, whose byte-identical multi-`--remove-label` leaf lives in providers/itp-github.sh (the CSV-extended `itp_github_transition_state`, OUTSIDE the scanned PIPELINE_FILES). So there is now NO remaining variable `--add/--remove-label "$var"` write in the pipeline files at all — `sites` is empty. The labels it strips are the hard-coded declared transitional set (modeled by the hygiene-strip-residue-* transitions), validated upstream, and the verb call carries only `$`-leading operands (skip-variable guard → no movement). NOTE (Form 3, spec-gate-itp): the scanner now ALSO recognizes a DIRECT `itp_transition_state "$n" "remove" "add"` call in a PIPELINE_FILE as a positional label-write site (token #1=issue ignored, #2=remove, #3=add; $-variable operands skipped); `norm()` splits the operand on `,`, so a CSV multi-label form ([INV-97]) emits its full movement. This keeps spec-gate coverage as #296 migrates `gh issue edit` → `itp_transition_state`: a migrated transition stays a scanned site (manifest entry kept, only the anchor may move within ±8 lines), so C.4 counts are coverage-preserving — no silent loss. The P1.1 variable-write ban is deliberately NOT extended to itp_transition_state: a variable-arg call (e.g. the label_swap / hygiene_strip delegations `itp_transition_state "$issue_num" "$remove" "$add"` / `"$remove_csv" ""`) carries no `--add/--remove-label` flag and emits no movement (its labels come from the literal label_swap callers / hard-coded jq allowlist C.1–C.5 already validate), so it is correctly invisible to both the movement scanners and P1.1.
